data.title: Trane Technologies beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Trane Technologies plc press release (TT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.86 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $5.15B (+5.7% Y/Y) beats by $60M.
  • Strong bookings of $5.8 billion, up 24 percent; organic bookings up 22 percent.
  • Record backlog of $7.8 billion, up 15 percent versus year-end 2024; Americas Commercial HVAC up 25 percent.
  • Book-to-bill was 112 percent, global Commercial HVAC book-to-bill was 114 percent.
    •Enterprise reported revenues were up 6 percent; organic revenues were up 4 percent.
    •GAAP operating margin was down 70 basis points, adjusted operating margin was flat and adjusted EBITDA margin was down 40 basis points.
  • Full-Year 2026 Guidance
    •The Company expects full-year 2026 reported revenue growth of approximately 8.5 percent to 9.5 percent (vs. estimated growth of 7.38%Y/Y); organic revenue growth of approximately 6 percent to 7 percent versus full-year 2025.
    •The Company expects GAAP and adjusted continuing EPS for full-year 2026 of $14.65 to $14.85 (vs. $14.79 consensus).
    •Additional information regarding the Company's 2026 guidance is included in the Company's earnings presentation found at www.tranetechnologies.com in the Investor Relations section.

data.title: Brunswick Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58 beats by $0.01, revenue of $1.33B beats by $120M
data.description:
• Brunswick press release (BC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $1.33B (+15.7% Y/Y)  beats by $120M.
  • 2026 Guidance: Net Sales Between $5.6 and $5.8 Billion vs $5.54B consensus; Adjusted Diluted EPS Range of $3.80 - $4.40 vs $4.19 consensus
  • First quarter 2026 revenue of between $1.2 to $1.4 billion, and adjusted diluted EPS of$0.35 to $0.45.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Thermo Fisher Scientific tops Q4 estimates; to provide FY26 guidance during call
Hennes & Mauritz reports FY results
data.description:
• Hennes & Mauritz press release (HNNMY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of SEK7.58.
  • Revenue of SEK228.29B (-2.6% Y/Y).
  • Gross profit amounted to SEK 121,821 m (125,299)
  • Operating profit increased to SEK 18,395 m (17,306), corresponding to an operating margin of 8.1 percent (7.4).
  • The result after tax increased to SEK 12,085 m (11,584), corresponding to SEK 7.58 (7.21) per share.
  • Cash flow from operating activities after changes in working capital amounted to SEK 31,120 m (31,756)

data.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Non-GAAP EPS of $6.57 beats by $0.12, revenue of $12.21B beats by $320M
data.description:
• Thermo Fisher Scientific press release (TMO): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $6.57 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $12.21B (+7.1% Y/Y) beats by $320M.
  • The company will provide 2026 financial guidance during its earnings conference call this morning at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time.

data.title: Dow Non-GAAP EPS of $1.81 beats by $2.32, revenue of $9.5B beats by $30M
data.description:
• Dow press release (DOW): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.81 beats by $2.32.
  • Revenue of $9.5B (-8.7% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Net sales were $9.5 billion, down 9% year-over-year, reflecting declines in all operating segments. Sequentially, net sales were down 5%, led by local price and volume declines from normal seasonality.
  • Volume decreased 2% year-over-year, led by declines in Packaging & Specialty Plastics, driven by lower merchant olefins sales in Europe, the Middle East, Africa and India (EMEAI) following the idling of a cracker in the region earlier in the year. Sequentially, volume decreased 2%, led by normal seasonality across building & construction.
  • Local price was down 8% versus the year-ago period and down 3% sequentially.
  •

data.title: Joby Aviation stock slips after pricing equity, convertible debt offerings
data.description:
Joby Aviation (JOBY) announced the pricing of its upsized offerings of stock and convertible senior notes.

The company will issue $600M of 0.75% convertible senior notes due 2032 and sell about 52.9M shares of common stock at $11.35 per share. In addition, Morgan Stanley will offer 5.28M borrowed shares in a separate “delta offering” to facilitate hedging transactions by some of the investors of the notes.

The notes will accrue interest at 0.75% per annum and will mature on February 15, 2032, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed, or converted. The initial conversion rate is 70.4846 shares of common stock per $1,000 principal amount of notes, which represents an initial conversion price of ~$14.19 per share.

The combined size of the note and stock offerings was increased from the previously announced $1.0B. The offerings are expected to settle on February 2, 2026, subject to closing conditions.

Joby also granted underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to an additional $90M more in notes and up to 7.9M additional shares.

The company expects to net about $576M from the common stock offering, or up to $662.4M if underwriters exercise their option to buy more shares. It also expects net proceeds of about $583M from the convertible note offering, rising to $670.4M if the over-allotment option is exercised.

About $55M of the note proceeds will be used for capped call transactions. The remaining funds, together with existing cash, will be used to support certification and manufacturing, prepare for commercial operations, and for general corporate purposes. Joby will receive no proceeds from the delta offering.

Shares were down 11.22% premarket.

data.title: STMicroelectronics forecasts Q1 revenue above estimates
data.description:
Shares of STMicroelectronics (STM) rose about 1% premarket on Thursday after the first quarter revenue outlook was seen above estimates.

Q4 Metrics 

Net revenues for the fourth quarter ended Dec. 31, 2025, grew 0.2% year-over-year to $3.33B, beating estimates. Non-U.S. GAAP EPS declined -70.3% year-over-year to $0.11.

"Q4 net revenues came above the mid-point of our business outlook range, driven by higher revenues in Personal Electronics and, to a lesser extent, in CECP and Industrial, while Automotive was below expectations," said STMicroelectronics' President and CEO Jean-Marc Chery. "Q4 revenues marked the return to year-over-year growth."

In the fourth quarter, the company posted operating income of $125M, including $141M related to impairment, restructuring charges, and other related phase-out costs. 

Analog products, MEMS and Sensors (AM&S) segment revenue grew 7.5% year-over-year to $1.45B, mainly due to Imaging, according to the company.

Revenue from the Power and Discrete products (P&D) segment decreased 31.6% year-over-year to $412M. Meanwhile, revenue from the Microcontrollers, Digital ICs and RF products ((MDRF)) Product Group rose 7% year-over-year to $1.46B.

Outlook

STMicroelectronics said net revenues for the first quarter of 2026 are expected to be $3.04B at the midpoint, a decrease of 8.7% sequentially, plus or minus 350 basis points. The consensus revenue estimate is $2.93B.

The company expects a gross margin of 33.7%, plus or minus 200 basis points, in the first quarter of 2026.

Related stocks: NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), Analog Devices (ADI), and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) each rose about +1% premarket on Thursday, while Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Texas Instruments (TXN) each dipped about 1%.

data.title: Gold tops $5500 per ounce
data.description:
Gold surged past $5,500 an ounce for the first time on Thursday, extending a rally driven by a weaker dollar and a broad investor retreat from sovereign bonds and currencies.

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) was up +1.7% at press time to $5,512. Silver (XAGUSD:CUR) (SLV) also hit a record high above $120 an ounce on Thursday.

Precious metals have surged this year amid heightened geopolitical tensions and concerns over the Federal Reserve’s independence. Spot gold is up nearly 30% this year, and silver has gained 67%, sharply accelerating multi-year bull markets and, due to the size and persistence of the rally, limiting banks’ ability to take positions.

Gold is also being lifted by the debasement trade, as worries over heavy fiscal spending and speculation that the U.S. could intervene to support the yen weigh on the dollar (DXY), making precious metals cheaper for most buyers. The Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index fell on Tuesday to its weakest level since March 2022, fueling gains in other global currencies.

U.S. President Donald Trump said this week he was unconcerned about the dollar’s decline, which has pushed the world’s main reserve currency to its weakest level in nearly four years. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent later struck a more supportive tone, saying the administration favors a strong dollar and has no plans to intervene by selling the currency against the yen.

data.title: Cuba's oil supplies seen lasting 15-20 days as shipments dry up - report
data.description:
Cuba has enough oil to only last 15-20 days at current levels of demand and domestic production as its sole remaining supplier Mexico appeared to cancel a shipment to the island, the Financial Times reported, citing data firm Kpler.

Oil accounts for most of the electricity generation in Cuba, which is already facing frequent power blackouts. Cuba received 84,900 barrels this year from a single Mexican shipment on Jan. 9.

If the shipment is added to an estimated 460,000 barrels held in inventories at the start of the year, "we can say that Cuba can run for 15-20 days," said Victoria Grabenwöger, crude research analyst at Kpler.

President Donald Trump has vowed to choke off Cuba's oil supplies. After the U.S. capture of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro, Mexico has emerged as the top oil supplier for the island.

Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Tuesday did not deny halting oil shipments to Cuba, saying, "It is a sovereign decision and it is made in the moment when necessary."

But on Wednesday, she clarified that she never said whether oil shipments to Cuba had been suspended or not. "Humanitarian aid to Cuba, as to other countries, continues," she said, which includes oil shipments.
